title: UBI: Machine Learning Framework for Variety Authentication and Geographic Provenance of Bohol Ubi 'Kinampay' (Dioscorea alata L.) Using EDXRF Elemental Fingerprinting
description:
Source dataset, complete analytical pipeline, and figure-generation code accompanying the manuscript: Machine Learning Framework for Variety Authentication and Geographic Provenance of Bohol Ubi 'Kinampay' (Dioscorea alata L.) Using EDXRF Elemental Fingerprinting, submitted to Scientific Reports.

The repository contains: (i) the source elemental composition dataset (52 samples × 7 elements + class labels) acquired by energy-dispersive X-ray fluorescence (EDXRF) on a Bruker S2 PUMA spectrometer; (ii) a Jupyter notebook implementing the full analytical pipeline including ten-algorithm benchmarking, leave-one-out cross-validation, Random Forest classification, and SHAP explainability analysis; and (iii) auxiliary scripts and dependency specifications for full reproducibility.

Headline result: Random Forest achieved 86.5% LOOCV accuracy (sensitivity = 94.4%, specificity = 68.8%, AUC = 0.888), with SHAP analysis identifying Mn (26.3%), Sr (17.2%), and Cu (16.3%) as the dominant geochemical predictors of authentic Bohol Kinampay variety.
